I'll never go hungry again, not if I have to steal or kill to prevent it.
Ashley, you must know that you're the only man I've ever loved or ever could love.
Great balls of fire! Don't bother me now about being a lady - I'm trying to save Tara.
Tomorrow I'll think of some way to get the money for those taxes, I'll think about it tomorrow.
I don't love you, Rhett Butler, and I don't care if you never come back.
Before God, I'll never be poor again - no matter what I have to do.
Fiddle-dee-dee! War, war, war - this talk of war is spoiling every party this spring.
I can shoot straight if I don't have to shoot too far - and I'm not afraid.
You're a gentleman and a scholar, Ashley Wilkes, but you don't know anything about business.
Mammy, I'm going to Atlanta whether you like it or not, and you can't stop me.
I'll marry Frank Kennedy if it's the last thing I do - Tara needs that money.
Don't you dare lecture me about what's proper - proper doesn't fill empty stomachs.
Ashley's worth any ten of you, Rhett Butler, and someday you'll realize it.
I swear by all that's holy, I'll never go hungry again, not if I have to steal or kill!
Ashley understands me in ways that Rhett Butler never could, no matter what anyone says.
These Atlanta ladies think they're so refined, but where were they when their world was burning down around them?
I didn't claw my way back from nothing just to let some banker take Tara away from me.
Tomorrow I'll figure out how to pay these taxes, but tonight I just can't think about it anymore.
Rhett, you're talking nonsense as usual - I could never love a man like you.
When I think of how we used to live before the war, with servants and plenty... but there's no use crying over spilled milk.
I'll do whatever it takes to keep food on our table, even if it means swallowing my pride.
Ashley may be married to Melanie, but she doesn't understand his sensitive nature like I do.
These hands weren't made for picking cotton, but I picked it anyway because someone had to.
I refuse to be one of those helpless women who sits around waiting for men to rescue them.
If marrying Frank Kennedy will save Tara, then I'll marry him and make the best of it.
Rhett Butler, you're a scoundrel and a cad, and I don't know why I waste my breath on you.
I've learned that you can't depend on anyone but yourself in this world.
The old days are gone, and crying about it won't bring them back - we have to make new ones.
I may not be a lady by their standards anymore, but at least I'm not starving.
Ashley's too good for this harsh world we're living in now - he needs someone to protect him.
Bonnie Blue, you're the light of my life, even if you are Rhett's daughter too.
I don't care what people say about my methods - results are what matter.
Mammy, stop your fussing - I know what I'm doing even if you don't approve.
This plantation will survive if I have to work my fingers to the bone to make it happen.
I'll think about the consequences tomorrow - today I have to act.
Let them gossip about me all they want - I'll still be standing when they've fallen by the wayside.
Charles was a sweet boy, but marrying him was about survival, not love.
I won't apologize for doing what I had to do to keep my family alive.
Rhett, your money can't buy you everything, especially not my heart.
Sometimes I dream about the barbecues at Twelve Oaks, but dreams don't put food on the table.
I've made mistakes, but I've also made sure none of us starved to death.
Ashley, if only you'd asked me to wait for you before you married Melanie.
I may have dirt under my fingernails now, but at least I have a roof over my head.
These new times call for new ways, and I'm not afraid to change with them.
Melanie's such a saint, but saints don't always understand what it takes to survive.
I'd rather be thought ruthless than watch everything I love slip away from me.
Great balls of fire, can't a woman make hard choices without everyone judging her?
The soil of Tara is worth more to me than all the fancy dresses in Atlanta.
I'll show them all that Scarlett O'Hara doesn't give up, no matter what they throw at me.
Fiddle-dee-dee, I'm tired of pretending to be something I'm not for their benefit.
If being a lady means being helpless, then I'd rather not be one.
I can't think about losing Ashley now - I'll go crazy if I do.
Every dollar I make is another step away from ever being hungry again.
Rhett sees right through me sometimes, and I hate him for it.
I've got to be strong for everyone - there's no one else to do it.
Those days of depending on men to take care of me are over forever.
As God is my witness, I'll rebuild everything we lost and more.
I won't let them see me break, no matter how tired I am of fighting.
Tomorrow will bring new problems, but also new possibilities.
I may not have Ashley's love, but I have his respect, and that's something.
Land is the only thing that lasts - everything else is just dreams and dust.
I'll face whatever comes next when the sun rises - tonight I just need peace.
After all, tomorrow is another day, and I'll be ready for whatever it brings.
I'll never go hungry again, not if I have to steal or kill - God is my witness!
Ashley, you must know that I've loved you since that day at Twelve Oaks.
I don't love you, Rhett Butler, and I never could love a man like you.
Tomorrow is another day, and I'll think of some way to get the money.
I won't think about that now - I'll think about it tomorrow when I can stand it.
Great balls of fire! Don't bother me anymore, and don't call me sugar!
I'll lie, steal, cheat, or kill - but I'll never be hungry again!
Fiddle-dee-dee! War, war, war - this talk of war is spoiling the fun at every party.
I can't let Tara go - it's the only thing I have left of Papa and the old days.
Ashley Wilkes, you're a gentleman and a scholar, but you don't know anything about women.
Rhett Butler, you're no gentleman - and you never pretend to be!
I'll show them all - I'll be the richest woman in Atlanta if it's the last thing I do.
Mammy, I don't want to hear any more of your sass about what's proper for a lady.
Melanie Hamilton is a pale-faced, mealy-mouthed nincompoop, but Ashley loves her.
I've got to get that three hundred dollars for the taxes, or we'll lose Tara.
Land is the only thing that matters - it's the only thing that lasts.
I'll marry him in the morning if he'll have me - I need that money for Tara.
Bonnie Butler, you're the most beautiful child in all of Atlanta, sugar.
I won't be poor white trash - I'd rather die first than live like that again.
With God as my witness, I swear I'll never starve again!
Ashley doesn't love me the way I love him, but someday he will.
Rhett, I hate you and I'm going to keep on hating you until I die!
I'll think about losing him tomorrow - I can't think about it today.
As God is my witness, I'll never let my family go hungry again!
Great goodness! I declare, sometimes I think you're the most aggravating man alive!
I must save Tara - it's all we have left of the life before the war.
Ashley's too much of a gentleman to know what he really wants.
Captain Butler, you forget yourself - no true gentleman would speak so to a lady.
I'll be the belle of Atlanta again, just you wait and see.
Mammy's been with this family since before I was born - she knows what's best.
Sweet Melanie thinks everyone is good and noble - she's such a fool sometimes.
I need that lumber mill to pay for itself, or we'll all be in the poorhouse.
Marriage is a business arrangement - love has nothing to do with it.
I know I've done terrible things, but I had to do them to save my family.
I can't bear to think about it now - tomorrow I'll be strong enough.
I will never go hungry again, no matter what I have to do!
Ashley, why can't you see that we belong together?
I don't care what you think, Rhett - I never have and I never will!
Tomorrow's another day, and maybe things will look better then.
I refuse to think about that right now - I'll go crazy if I do.
Fiddlesticks! I'm tired of everyone telling me what I can and can't do.
Tara is worth any sacrifice - it's our home and our heritage.
Ashley Wilkes is a dreamer who lives in the past instead of facing reality.
You're a cad and a scoundrel, Rhett Butler, and I despise you!
I'll prove to all of Atlanta that I'm still a force to be reckoned with.
Poor dear Mammy - she tries so hard to make a lady out of me.
Melly is the only truly good person I know, and I've treated her shamefully.
If marrying him will save Tara, then I'll walk down that aisle tomorrow.
I've always taken care of myself, and I always will - I don't need anyone.
After all, tomorrow is another day!
I swear by all that's holy, I'll never let poverty touch me or mine again!
Ashley, you must know that my feelings haven't changed despite everything that's happened.
These Atlanta ladies think they're so refined, but I've built something they could never manage.
I don't need anyone's charity or pity - I can take care of myself and Tara too.
The old days are gone forever, and crying about it won't bring them back.
Bonnie, you're the light of my life, even when you're being impossible.
Mammy, you worry too much about what's proper when there's work to be done.
Melanie, your kindness puts the rest of us to shame, though I'd never admit it publicly.
Marriage can be a practical arrangement when it serves the right purposes.
Everything I've done has been necessary - I won't apologize for surviving.
Why dwell on today's troubles when tomorrow might bring better fortune?
I'll show them all that Scarlett O'Hara doesn't know the meaning of defeat.
As God is my witness, I'll claw my way back to the top if I have to!
Ashley understands me in ways that others simply cannot.
Let those society matrons gossip - I've accomplished what they never could.
Nobody was coming to rescue me, so I learned to rescue myself.
Before the war, everything was beautiful and we were all so innocent.
Bonnie has your spirit, Rhett, though I hate to give you the credit.
Mammy raised me better than my own mother ever could have.
Dear, sweet Melanie sees good in everyone, even when it's not there.
Sometimes a woman must marry for security rather than silly romantic notions.
I did what had to be done, and I'd do it all again without hesitation.
There's no point worrying about it now - I'll face it when the time comes.
I'll prove to every last person in this city that I belong here.
Hunger is a specter that haunts me still, and I won't let it return.
Ashley, surely you can see that we're meant to be together.
These people may have money now, but they lack my determination.
I've always been able to handle things on my own when push comes to shove.
The plantation life we knew is just a memory now, beautiful but gone.
My daughter deserves everything wonderful this world can offer.
Mammy, you've been more constant than anyone else in my life.
Melanie's loyalty is something I've never quite understood but always valued.
A smart woman uses every advantage she has, including marriage if necessary.
My conscience is clear because I fought for what mattered most.
Why torture myself with today's problems when tomorrow is a fresh start?
I'll rise above every obstacle they put in my path, mark my words.
I swear on my father's grave, poverty will never darken Tara's door again!
Ashley, you must realize that true love doesn't simply disappear.
They can whisper behind their fans all they want - I know my own worth.
When everyone else gave up, I rolled up my sleeves and got to work.
Those golden days before the war seem like a dream now.
Bonnie, you're going to have every advantage I can give you in this world.
Mammy knows me better than I know myself sometimes.
Sweet Melanie could find virtue in the devil himself if she tried.
Marriage is a tool like any other when a woman needs to secure her future.
I've done nothing that any reasonable person wouldn't do in my position.
What's the use of fretting over spilled milk? Tomorrow brings new possibilities.
I'll show this whole city that Scarlett O'Hara is a force to be reckoned with.
Rhett, you know perfectly well that my heart belongs to someone else.
After all we've been through, tomorrow has to be better than today.
